display.purpose: Hero name only. Orbitron 900, set huge and tight. Carries the glitch layers.

headline.purpose: Section titles, with teal accent spans on emphasis words.

title.purpose: Project card titles and stat values.

body.purpose: Rajdhani paragraphs; strong lifts to HUD White, em shifts to teal.

label.purpose: Share Tech Mono, uppercase, tracked. Every code-like element, nav, badge, eyebrow. Carries // and > prefixes.

shadows.name: status-glow
shadows.purpose: Neon bloom on the pulsing status dot. The only sanctioned box-shadow; never used as elevation.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default easing for transitions, entrances, and reveals. The strong custom curve that gives motion punch.

motion.name: press
motion.purpose: Press feedback on all pressable elements.

motion.name: glitch-name
motion.purpose: Hero name signal-tear glitch (3 layers: yellow base + teal + red channels).

motion.name: cursor-lerp
motion.purpose: Lagging cursor ring; both GPU transform-driven.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Solid yellow CTA with the signature angled clip-path cut. Lead action.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: 'Share Tech Mono', monospace; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; font-weight: 600; padding: 14px 32px; background: #f5e642; color: #0a0c14; border: none; cursor: pointer; clip-path: polygon(8px 0%, 100% 0%, calc(100% - 8px) 100%, 0% 100%); transition: background 0.2s, transform 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #fff7a0; transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(0) scale(0.97); transition-duration: 0.1s; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Transparent secondary action with glass border; hover shifts to teal.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { font-family: 'Share Tech Mono', monospace; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 13px 31px; background: transparent; color: #e8eaf0; border: 1px solid rgba(245,230,66,0.4); cursor: pointer; clip-path: polygon(8px 0%, 100% 0%, calc(100% - 8px) 100%, 0% 100%); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); transition: border-color 0.2s, color 0.2s, transform 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: #00e5c8; color: #00e5c8; transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-ghost:active { transform: translateY(0) scale(0.97); transition-duration: 0.1s; }

components.name: Glass Panel
components.description: Sharp-cornered glass card over the atmosphere stack: yellow top-edge highlight, hover lift, L-shaped corner accent.
components.html:
FEATURED · UI DESIGN
Project Zero

The one you lead with. The one that makes people stop scrolling.
components.css: .ds-card { position: relative; overflow: hidden; padding: 28px; background: rgba(8,14,28,0.55); border: 1px solid rgba(245,230,66,0.18); backdrop-filter: blur(20px); color: #e8eaf0; transition: border-color 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.23,1,0.32,1), transform 0.45s cubic-bezier(0.23,1,0.32,1); } .ds-card::before { content: ''; position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; height: 1px; background: linear-gradient(90deg, transparent, #f5e642, transparent); opacity: 0.5; } .ds-card:hover { border-color: rgba(245,230,66,0.4); transform: translateY(-4px); } .ds-card-corner { position: absolute; top: 0; right: 0; width: 40px; height: 40px; border-top: 2px solid #f5e642; border-right: 2px solid #f5e642; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 0.3s; } .ds-card:hover .ds-card-corner { opacity: 1; } .ds-card-type { font-family: 'Share Tech Mono', monospace; font-size: 10px; letter-spacing: 0.15em; color: #00e5c8; text-transform: uppercase; margin-bottom: 12px; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Orbitron', monospace; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-card-desc { font-family: 'Rajdhani', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.6; color: rgba(232,234,240,0.5); }

components.name: Badge
components.description: Mono uppercase status/skill tag; active variant turns teal.
components.html: ▶ AVAILABLE FOR WORK UI / UX
components.css: .ds-badge { font-family: 'Share Tech Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.1em; padding: 5px 14px; border: 1px solid rgba(245,230,66,0.18); color: rgba(232,234,240,0.5); background: rgba(0,0,0,0.3); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); } .ds-badge-active { border-color: #00e5c8; color: #00e5c8; }

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Lowercase mono nav link; hover lifts to HUD White and reveals a teal > prefix.
components.css: .ds-nav-link { font-family: 'Share Tech Mono', monospace; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.1em; color: rgba(232,234,240,0.5); text-decoration: none; text-transform: lowercase; transition: color 0.2s; } .ds-nav-link::before { content: '> '; color: #00e5c8; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 0.2s; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #e8eaf0; } .ds-nav-link:hover::before { opacity: 1; }

components.name: Status Indicator
components.description: Pulsing teal dot with neon glow plus a mono status readout. The signature 'alive' element.
components.css: .ds-status { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-family: 'Share Tech Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; color: #00e5c8; } .ds-status-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: #00e5c8; box-shadow: 0 0 8px #00e5c8; animation: ds-pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-pulse { 0%,100% { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); } 50% { opacity: 0.4; transform: scale(0.7); } }

narrative.overview: This is not a portfolio that happens to look cyberpunk. It is a Night City netrunner's heads-up display that happens to be a portfolio. Every surface behaves like a panel in an operator's interface: glass readouts floating over a live video feed, status tickers cycling, section labels prefixed with // like console output, and the hero name glitching as if the signal can't quite hold it steady. The interface is diegetic; the visitor isn't reading a resume, they're jacked into someone's profile. The mood is electric, defiant, cinematic. Depth comes from a dark, near-black field lit by two neon signals: an aggressive electric yellow and a colder teal. Atmosphere is structural, never decorative. Red exists only as a wound: glitch interference and danger, nothing else. This system rejects corporate/LinkedIn-bland, the generic SaaS template, and the all-white minimalist portfolio. Loud is the brand; the only constraint is legibility.
keyCharacteristics: Diegetic terminal/HUD framing: // labels, system readouts, status tickers
Near-black canvas with twin neon accents (yellow primary, teal secondary)
Glass panels over a live video background; sharp angled geometry, never rounded
Motion as identity: glitch, scanlines, grain, lagging cursor, scroll reveals
Orbitron display set huge and tight; mono for every label and code-like element

rules.name: The Two-Signal Rule
rules.body: Yellow leads, teal answers, and they never swap jobs: yellow is the thing you act on, teal is the thing that's alive (status, links, motion).

rules.name: The Gray-Is-Banned Rule
rules.body: There is no flat gray. Muted text is always HUD White at reduced opacity, so it stays tinted to the canvas.

rules.name: The Red-Is-A-Wound Rule
rules.body: Red is danger and interference only, used exclusively in the glitch effect's red channel. Never for normal UI, text, or accents.

rules.name: The Console-Label Rule
rules.body: Every label, nav item, tag, badge, and section eyebrow is Share Tech Mono, uppercase, tracked, most with a // or > prefix. The deliberate terminal voice, applied consistently.

rules.name: The Display-Is-For-Shouting Rule
rules.body: Orbitron is reserved for the hero name, section titles, and numeric stat values. Never set body copy in Orbitron.

rules.name: The No-Drop-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Surfaces never cast soft gray drop shadows. Depth comes from backdrop-filter blur, the top-edge yellow highlight, and layered atmosphere behind glass.

rules.name: The Ghost-Card Ban
rules.body: Never pair a 1px border with a wide soft box-shadow on the same element. Panels get a glass border OR a glow, never a lifted-card shadow.

dos: Do keep every label, nav item, tag, and section eyebrow in Share Tech Mono, uppercase, with // or > prefixes. The terminal voice is the brand.
Do use the angled clip-path cut on buttons (polygon(8px 0%, 100% 0%, calc(100% - 8px) 100%, 0% 100%)). The diagonal corner is intentional.
Do build depth with backdrop-filter glass, the top-edge yellow highlight, and the layered video/scanline/grain/vignette stack, never drop shadows.
Do use yellow as the action color and teal as the alive/status color (The Two-Signal Rule).
Do render muted text as HUD White at reduced opacity, never a flat gray (The Gray-Is-Banned Rule).
Do preserve the scanlines, film grain, vignette, custom cursor, glitch name, and video background. They are structural identity.
Do use --ease-out: cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1) for transitions and add scale(0.97) press feedback to every pressable element.
donts: Don't drift toward corporate / LinkedIn-bland: no beige, no safe, no personality-free layouts. The single worst failure mode.
Don't introduce the generic SaaS/startup look: no Inter, no soft purple gradients, no rounded cards, no modern-minimal defaults.
Don't build the all-white minimalist designer portfolio: thin gray type on white with no point of view is forbidden.
Don't round corners on cards, panels, or buttons. Geometry is sharp; only the cursor, dots, and rings are circular.
Don't use Glitch Red for anything except the glitch effect's red channel. Red is a wound, not an accent.
Don't pair a 1px border with a wide soft box-shadow (the ghost-card pattern), and don't add generic lifted-card drop shadows.
Don't set body copy or long strings in Orbitron; reserve the display face for the hero name, section titles, and stat numbers.
Don't let spectacle bury the message: the name, role, work, and contact path must stay instantly legible under all the motion.
